phase: milestone-close + pollen-release (cross-cutting; not a single phase)
phase_name: v1.3.0 milestone close (PAUSED) + Pollen v0.2.0 release (DONE)
milestone_context: ALL v1.3.0 phases (10-21) are done. The Phase-20 20-02 LlamaFirewall HF-license gate was CLOSED this session. The standalone Pollen tool was license-cleared, coverage-hardened, and RELEASED as v0.2.0 (signed + SBOM + SLSA, public). The ONLY remaining tracked item is the v1.3.0 milestone close, which is PAUSED at its confirmation gate (readiness diagnostics already gathered).

completed_tasks.name: Phase-20 20-02 LlamaFirewall HF-license gate CLOSED (gated-model download verified on Windows; full sidecar/e2e is CI/Linux-only by design — codeshield->semgrep has no Windows build)
Pollen license verdict: Apache-2.0 derivative, SAFE to release (verified perplexityai/bumblebee is Apache-2.0 at pinned v0.1.1)
Pollen coverage quick task 260611-mpe (model 100/walk 84.8/scanner 79.6/editorext 98.5/cmd-pollen 80.4 + internal/coveragegate VAL-01-style gate)
beekeeper scanner fix: prefer bumblebee, fall back to pollen (was pollen-only on all OSes)
Docs positioning (Bumblebee=mac/linux upstream; Pollen=Windows stopgap; Beekeeper inspired-by) + Apache-2.0 LICENSE for beekeeper

Pollen v0.2.0 RELEASED (single clean semver, scrapped the 4-tag pollen.2-.5 scheme): signed cosign + SLSA + CycloneDX SBOM + 6 platform binaries; main CI green after a backslash-test fix; beekeeper pin -> v0.2.0

remaining_tasks.name: Run /gsd-complete-milestone v1.3.0 (PAUSED at confirmation gate; readiness diagnostics already done)
OPTIONAL: cosign verify-blob the v0.2.0 release locally; surface the v0.2.0 binary in install docs

human_actions_pending.action: Decide whether to run /gsd-complete-milestone v1.3.0 now (archives REQUIREMENTS.md + rewrites ROADMAP.md + tags v1.3.0 locally)
human_actions_pending.context: Destructive but git-checkpointed; the readiness picture is already gathered (see continue-here)

decisions.decision: beekeeper resolves scanner bumblebee->pollen on ALL OSes (not pollen-only)
decisions.rationale: bumblebee is recommended mac/linux; pollen is the Windows fallback (bumblebee has no Windows build)
matches Bumblebee/Pollen lineage + patent grant; beekeeper is original work (subprocess boundary, no code copied)

decisions.decision: Pollen §4(b) = repo-level NOTICE statement, NOT per-file headers
decisions.rationale: module-path rewrite makes the modification pervasive across all files; per-file would be noise

decisions.decision: Single v0.2.0 release instead of the 4 batched pollen.2/.3/.4/.5 tags
decisions.rationale: the -pollen.N scheme was a GSD per-phase-tag artifact, not a real cadence; clean semver is simpler

next_action: Confirm with the maintainer, then run /gsd-complete-milestone v1.3.0. Preserve the parked v1.1.0 Pollen section in ROADMAP.md (do the ROADMAP reorg BY HAND, not the generic CLI rewrite). REQUIREMENTS.md is cleanly v1.3.0-only so archive+git-rm is safe. No git remote on beekeeper (local-only) so no tag push. No v1.3.0-MILESTONE-AUDIT.md exists (every phase was individually verified; the 4 audit-open items are stale; SITE-03 Vercel deploy is the one intentional deferral).
context_notes: Massive session. Started by resuming the Phase-20 LlamaFirewall gate, closed it, then the maintainer pivoted to the real blocker for the parked v1.1.0: whether releasing the Pollen fork is license-permissible. It is (Apache-2.0). That unblocked a full Pollen release sprint: coverage -> license/docs hardening -> bumblebee-first scanner fix -> public push -> v0.2.0 signed release. Pollen is now a public, signed, standalone Windows-focused tool. Beekeeper itself is unpushed/local-only (no remote). The v1.3.0 milestone close is the only loose thread, deliberately paused for the maintainer's go-ahead. Reusable lesson burned in this session: filepath.ToSlash only rewrites the HOST-OS separator, so backslash-path test assertions pass on Windows but fail on Linux/macOS CI.
